title: Plugin manifest schema (zod) + JSON Schema artifact
description: Define the canonical `roubo-plugin.yaml` manifest shape in `shared/plugin-manifest-schema.ts` as a zod schema covering id, name, version, roubo (host API range), kind: 'integration', entry, configSchema (JSON Schema for user config), and the four permission categories (network.hosts, credentials.slots, filesystem.paths, processes). Publish a matching `schema/roubo-plugin.schema.json` artifact so editors validate YAML. Reject manifests with missing or invalid required fields; reject unknown fields with a clear error. Existing roubo.yaml files (no integration block) must continue to validate.
acceptance_criteria: shared/plugin-manifest-schema.ts exports a zod schema with the four permission categories and required fields
schema/roubo-plugin.schema.json exists and mirrors the zod schema for IDE tooling
Manifest with all four permission categories parses successfully
Manifest with a missing required field is rejected with a clear error naming the field
Unknown top-level fields are rejected by .strict()
Test coverage from TC-006 passes

title: NormalizedIssue contract + bench-snapshot integration fields
description: Extend `shared/types.ts` with the new `NormalizedIssue` shape (integrationId, externalId, externalUrl, title, body, currentState, allowedTransitions, assignees, labels, issueType, blocks, blockedBy, updatedAt, raw). Add `integrationId` and `externalId` fields to the bench snapshot (`AssignedIssue` / `PersistedBench`). Provide a migration path that defaults pre-migration benches to integrationId: 'github-com' and stringifies the legacy numeric `number` to `externalId`. Update server-side consumers (`server/services/state.ts`, `server/services/issue-assignment.ts`) to read/write the new fields without breaking existing callers.
acceptance_criteria: NormalizedIssue type exported from shared/types.ts with all listed fields
AssignedIssue / PersistedBench carry integrationId + externalId (string)
Pre-migration benches default to integrationId: 'github-com' and externalId derived from legacy 'number'
Server compiles without breaking existing consumers (adapter where needed)
Test coverage from TC-024, TC-032 passes

title: roubo.yaml integration block + zod + JSON Schema update
description: Add the new optional `integration` block to the roubo.yaml zod schema (`shared/config-schema.ts`) with field-level optionality: `plugin`, `instance`, `sources`, and `pluginSource` (Git URL or local path hint for missing-plugin prompts). Update `schema/roubo-config.schema.json` to match. Existing roubo.yaml files without an integration block must continue to validate. Wire `server/services/config-parser.ts` to surface the new block in the parsed config.
acceptance_criteria: shared/config-schema.ts accepts the integration block with every subfield optional
schema/roubo-config.schema.json mirrors the zod additions
Existing roubo.yaml files (no integration block) validate unchanged
Unknown subfields under integration are rejected per the existing .strict() pattern
Test coverage from TC-026 (input parse path) and TC-067 passes

title: Per-user override file + effective-config deep-merge resolver
description: Define the per-user override file at `~/.roubo/integrations/<projectId>.yaml` with envelope `{ schemaVersion: 1, integration: {...} }` and its own zod schema. Implement a deep-merge resolver that combines the committed roubo.yaml integration block with the user override per field; arrays REPLACE (not concat). Expose the effective integration config via a server service consumed by routes and the UI. Implementation note: prefer a small hand-rolled deep-merge over a heavy lib, given the field set is tiny.
acceptance_criteria: ~/.roubo/integrations/<projectId>.yaml format + zod schema landed
Effective-config resolver merges roubo.yaml + override per field (deep merge)
Array fields (sources.boards / sources.repos / etc.) REPLACE on override
Empty array in override replaces a non-empty roubo.yaml array
Resolver exposed as a server service consumed by routes
Test coverage from TC-026, TC-027, TC-065 passes

title: Plugin host supervisor + JSON-RPC over stdio (lifecycle, timeout, restart, logs)
description: Implement `server/services/plugin-manager.ts` as the plugin process supervisor. Discover plugins under bundled `plugins/` and `~/.roubo/plugins/<plugin-id>/`. Spawn each enabled plugin as a child Node process via `child_process.spawn` with `vscode-jsonrpc` framed messages over stdio. Implement enable / disable / restart lifecycle hooks. Enforce a per-call RPC timeout (default 30s, plugin-method-overridable). Implement auto-restart with exponential backoff capped at 3 attempts per 5-minute rolling window per plugin; mark `errored` after budget exceeded. Write stdout/stderr to `~/.roubo/plugins/<id>/logs/current.log` with size-based rotation to `previous.log`. Refuse incompatible plugins (manifest `roubo` range mismatch). Continue boot if any single plugin fails to load. Tear down children cleanly on host SIGTERM and rely on POSIX SIGHUP-on-parent-exit for hard crashes (macOS + Linux only).
acceptance_criteria: plugin-manager.ts loads bundled + ~/.roubo/plugins/ at boot
Each enabled plugin runs as an isolated child Node process
JSON-RPC over stdio framed via vscode-jsonrpc
Per-call timeout (default 30s) cancels hanging calls without orphaning the child
Auto-restart bounded by 3-in-5-minutes per plugin; status flips to errored after budget exceeded
stdout / stderr written to ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>/logs/current.log; rotated to previous.log by size
Incompatible plugins shown as 'incompatible' on Plugins page; not spawned
Host boot completes even if zero plugins load
Host shutdown tears down children within 5s; no zombies on macOS + Linux
Test coverage from TC-001 through TC-005, TC-013, TC-014, TC-015, TC-033, TC-073, TC-074, TC-077, TC-078 passes

title: host.fetch HTTP client (allowlist, proxy, self-signed opt-in, header passthrough)
description: Implement `server/services/plugin-http.ts` as the host-provided HTTP client plugins call via the RPC channel. Use `undici` with an `EnvHttpProxyAgent` honouring `HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY` / `NO_PROXY`. Enforce the plugin manifest's `network.hosts` glob allowlist before dispatch. Allow a per-plugin-instance self-signed TLS opt-in via a custom Dispatcher with `rejectUnauthorized: false`. Surface raw response headers (ETag, Retry-After, X-RateLimit-*) verbatim across the RPC boundary so plugin-internal caching and backoff remain implementable.
acceptance_criteria: host.fetch dispatches via undici with EnvHttpProxyAgent
Manifest network.hosts allowlist enforced before dispatch; out-of-allowlist URLs rejected with structured error
Per-plugin self-signed TLS toggle uses a custom Dispatcher with rejectUnauthorized: false when enabled
Raw response headers (ETag, Retry-After, X-RateLimit-*) surfaced verbatim to the plugin
Denials logged with stable pluginId.methodName identifier
Test coverage from TC-008, TC-009, TC-010 passes

title: Credential store (pure-JS shellout, macOS + Linux) with slot-scope enforcement
description: Implement `server/services/credential-store.ts` that stores plugin credentials in the OS keyring via pure-JS shellouts: `security` on macOS (Keychain), `secret-tool` on Linux (Secret Service / gnome-keyring). NO native modules. NO Windows path (Roubo does not support Windows). Expose `host.credentials.get(slot)` and `host.credentials.set(slot, value)` as RPC methods. Enforce that a plugin can only read/write slots it declared in its manifest's credentials.slots; deny others with a structured error. Document the Ubuntu headless recipe (`dbus-run-session` + `gnome-keyring-daemon --start --components=secrets`) in the credential-store module's README and in architecture.md.
acceptance_criteria: credential-store.ts implements set/get via `security` on macOS and `secret-tool` on Linux
No native modules added to dependencies
host.credentials.get / set are exposed via RPC and slot-scope enforced
Plugin reading a slot it did not declare receives a structured permission error; nothing leaks
Plaintext credential never appears in any file under ~/.roubo/
Ubuntu headless recipe documented in module README + architecture.md
Test coverage from TC-011, TC-012, TC-070 passes; TC-083 covers the headless recipe manually

title: Permission enforcement: filesystem + child-process categories + cross-category tests
description: Complete the four-permission-category model started in WU-001/006/007 by enforcing the filesystem and child-process categories: confine each plugin to its own directory by default, deny declared filesystem paths if the host detects access outside them, deny child-process spawning unless declared. Document the limitation that a plugin process could still bypass host helpers and use raw `fs` / `child_process` directly (a documented trust boundary; the host audits via process monitoring where feasible). Add cross-category enforcement tests.
acceptance_criteria: Filesystem access category enforced via host helper (host.fs.* if exposed) or as a documented best-effort boundary
Child-process category enforced via host helper or documented boundary
Bypass-via-raw-fs limitation documented in the SDK / plugin author docs
Test coverage from TC-080 passes; TC-070 cross-checked

title: Plugin SDK package (@roubo/plugin-sdk) + author docs
description: Ship `@roubo/plugin-sdk` (initially under `shared/plugin-sdk/` or `plugins/sdk/`; final package name decided during build). Export `definePlugin({...})` that wires stdio binding via vscode-jsonrpc and binds host helpers (`host.fetch`, `host.credentials.get`, `host.credentials.set`, `host.logger.{info,warn,error}`). Plugin author writes only contract methods; the SDK handles the RPC framing. Include author docs covering manifest format, contract methods (listSourceCandidates, listIssues, getIssue, getCurrentUser, validateConfig, applyTransition, assignIssue, unassignIssue), pagination shape, error shapes, and the trust boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: @roubo/plugin-sdk exports definePlugin and host helper bindings
A reference plugin (in tests fixtures) imports the SDK and runs end-to-end
Author docs cover manifest, all contract methods, error shapes, and pagination
Test coverage from TC-035 passes

title: Plugins settings page (list, status, View logs)
description: New top-level 'Plugins' settings tab. Render installed plugins as cards with name, version, status pill (enabled / disabled / errored / incompatible), source label ('Bundled' or '~/.roubo/plugins/<id>/'), description, and an action row (Configure, View logs, Enable/Disable, Uninstall for third-party only). Show errored cards with a red banner ('Plugin failed to start after 3 restart attempts. Showing your last successful issue snapshot.') and inline Restart + View logs. Show incompatible cards with an amber banner. Empty third-party state with an 'Install plugin' CTA. View logs opens a wide React Aria `Dialog` showing recent log content with timestamp + level + message; no new Drawer primitive this slug.
acceptance_criteria: Plugins settings tab present at top level
Cards render status pill, source label, action row per the prototype mockup (screen 1)
Errored / incompatible variants render their banners
View logs Dialog displays current.log and previous.log with size-rotated content
Search filter inside the log Dialog works
Page is keyboard-navigable per Roubo accessibility conventions
Test coverage from TC-001, TC-002, TC-003, TC-013, TC-016, TC-017, TC-018 passes; TC-046 (keyboard nav) passes

title: Install plugin dialog + permissions acceptance (Git URL + local directory)
description: Install plugin Dialog with two tabs (Git URL, Local directory). On Install: clone Git URL via `git clone` shellout into `~/.roubo/plugins/<id>/`, or validate the local directory contains a valid `roubo-plugin.yaml`. After manifest validation, show the permissions Dialog listing every declared permission category with detail (network host globs, credential slots, filesystem paths, child-process flag), plus the install source URL or absolute path. Install proceeds only after user acceptance. Cancel reverts without partial state.
acceptance_criteria: Install Dialog with Git URL + local directory tabs
Git URL flow shells out to `git clone` into ~/.roubo/plugins/<id>/
Local dir flow validates roubo-plugin.yaml presence + parses manifest
Failures surface as inline red banners (unreachable URL, missing manifest, incompatible host range); no partial install
Permissions Dialog lists every declared permission category + the install source
Cancel from permissions Dialog reverts cleanly (no orphan files / state)
Test coverage from TC-007, TC-019, TC-058, TC-059, TC-075 passes

title: Per-plugin configure dialog + Test connection + identity capture
description: Render the plugin's `configSchema` as a form (TextField, Checkbox, password fields). Add a `Test connection` button that calls `plugin.validateConfig(config)` and an inline result strip showing success ('Connected as <Display Name>') / auth error / network error / TLS error variants. On TLS error, offer an inline 'Enable self-signed TLS' button that flips the toggle and re-runs. On Save, call `plugin.getCurrentUser(config)` once, capture the external user id, and persist it to the per-user override; Save is gated by a successful Test connection for the current values (dialog-local React state `hasTestedSuccessfully`, reset on any field change). Save writes to the per-user override file, NOT roubo.yaml.
acceptance_criteria: Configure Dialog renders form fields driven by the plugin's manifest configSchema
Test connection button calls validateConfig and renders all four result-strip states
TLS-error variant offers an inline 'Enable self-signed TLS' button that re-runs the test
Save calls getCurrentUser once and stores the captured external id on the per-user override
Save is enabled only after a successful Test connection for the current values (dialog-local hasTestedSuccessfully state, reset on field change)
Save writes to ~/.roubo/integrations/<projectId>.yaml (override), not roubo.yaml
Test coverage from TC-037, TC-038, TC-060, TC-061, TC-062 passes

title: Issue source tile + Switch integration dialog + active-bench badge
description: Per-project 'Issue source' tile on the project detail page. Show configured state (active integration pill, instance URL, selected sources grouped by category, effective-config caption, Configure / Switch integration actions). Show unconfigured state with 'Choose integration' CTA. Show missing-plugin variant with 'Install plugin' CTA. Switch integration Dialog: radio list of installed integrations, confirmation explaining bench survival behaviour, on confirm: writes new integration choice to per-user override. Active-bench badge: existing benches gain an 'Issue from previous integration' pill when the project's active integration no longer matches their snapshot; source-sync disabled for those benches.
acceptance_criteria: Issue source tile renders configured / unconfigured / missing-plugin variants
Effective-config caption reads accurately per source (roubo.yaml only, override only, or both)
Switch integration Dialog confirms before writing the change
Switch writes to per-user override; roubo.yaml untouched
Active benches against a now-inactive integration render the 'Issue from previous integration' badge
Source-sync controls disabled on those benches
Test coverage from TC-020, TC-028 (variant), TC-052, TC-056 passes

title: Declarative source picker renderer (multi-list + categorized-multi-list)
description: Build `client/src/components/SourcePicker.tsx` switching on the shape returned by `plugin.listSourceCandidates`. `multi-list`: single combined list with type labels and search (used by GitHub.com + GHE). `categorized-multi-list`: React Aria `Tabs` over Boards / Epics / Filters, each tab with its own search and selection (used by Jira). Selected items render as removable chips below the list. Reuse the existing `client/src/components/MultiSelect.tsx` primitive. No plugin React.
acceptance_criteria: SourcePicker.tsx renders both shapes from a single component, switching on shape
multi-list: combined repos + Projects list with type label, search, chip strip
categorized-multi-list: tabs per category, search and selection per tab, chip strip groups by category
Keyboard navigation works: Tab order is sane, arrow keys navigate items, Space/Enter selects
Test coverage from TC-021, TC-022, TC-076 passes

title: Missing-plugin prompt on project load
description: When a project's effective integration references a plugin id not installed locally, show a 'Plugin needed for this project' Dialog with three resolution paths: (a) if `roubo.yaml.integration.pluginSource` is set, offer a one-click Install from that source, (b) otherwise let the user paste a Git URL or local path, (c) Skip for now (project loads with the Issue source tile's missing-plugin variant). Install proceeds via the Install permissions Dialog from WU-011, then the project loads.
acceptance_criteria: Missing-plugin Dialog appears on project load when plugin id is referenced but not installed
pluginSource hint from roubo.yaml drives a one-click Install when present
Without a hint, user can paste Git URL or local path
Skip for now is supported; tile shows missing-plugin variant
Install completes and project loads normally
Test coverage from TC-028, TC-051, TC-066 passes

title: Issue retrieval routes through active plugin (paginated, dedup, issue types)
description: Re-route `server/routes/issues.ts` (`GET /:projectId/issues`, `GET /:projectId/issues/:externalId`, `GET /:projectId/issues/:externalId/comments`, `GET /:projectId/labels`, `GET /:projectId/project-items`) to call the active integration plugin's methods instead of `githubService.*`. Implement paginated listIssues consumption: server forwards `{ pageSize, cursor }` to the plugin and returns `{ items, nextCursor }`; client uses React Query's `useInfiniteQuery`. Dedup across sources keyed on `(integrationId, externalId)`. Update `GET /:projectId/issue-types` to source from the active plugin. Update `IssueQueuePanel`, `IssuePickerModal`, `DraggableIssueCard` to consume `NormalizedIssue`. Detect and stop misbehaving plugins that return non-progressing cursors.
acceptance_criteria: All issue routes route through the active integration plugin
listIssues is paginated; default page size 50; plugin-overridable via config
Client uses useInfiniteQuery; UI does not block on any page
Cross-source dedup applied keyed on (integrationId, externalId)
Issue-type strings sourced from the active plugin; mapping persistence shape Record<string, string> preserved
Misbehaving paginator (unchanging cursor) detected; inline UI note shown; no infinite loop
Server compiles; client renders without referencing GitHub-only fields
Test coverage from TC-023, TC-025, TC-033, TC-057, TC-071, TC-079 passes

title: Soft-block flip: blocks/blocked-by becomes a warning, not a refusal
description: Flip `server/services/issue-assignment.ts:102` (`checkIssueDependencies`) from a hard refusal to a warning surface. Update `client/src/components/IssuePickerModal.tsx:91` in lockstep to render an amber-tinted banner naming the open blockers but leave the Create bench button enabled. Update related test fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: server/services/issue-assignment.ts:102 returns an advisory result, not a refusal
client/src/components/IssuePickerModal.tsx:91 renders the amber banner; Create bench button stays enabled
Bench creation succeeds even with open blockers
Existing unit / integration tests are updated to match the new behaviour
Test coverage from TC-034 passes

title: Bench-view: Transition dropdown + applyTransition wiring
description: Add a 'Transition to' control in the bench's issue view rendering the issue's `allowedTransitions` array. On select, call `plugin.applyTransition(externalId, transitionName)` via RPC with optimistic UI (state pill flips immediately). On error from the plugin (structured), revert the optimistic update and show an inline red banner with the plugin's error message verbatim. Do NOT auto-fire transitions on bench lifecycle events (creation, start, clear, PR merge).
acceptance_criteria: Transition control populated only from issue.allowedTransitions
Empty allowedTransitions array shows 'No transitions available from this state.'
Selecting a transition optimistically updates the UI and calls plugin.applyTransition
Plugin errors revert the optimistic update and surface inline
No applyTransition call is sent for bench lifecycle events
On host crash mid-flight: no persisted in-flight state; next refresh reconciles from source
Test coverage from TC-039, TC-054, TC-063, TC-064 passes

title: Bench-view: Assign / Unassign control
description: Add 'Assign to me' / 'Unassign me' controls in the bench's issue view. On Assign-to-me, call `plugin.assignIssue(externalId, capturedUserId)` using the captured identity from the per-user override. On Unassign, call `plugin.unassignIssue(externalId)`. Optimistic UI; revert + inline error on structured failure.
acceptance_criteria: Assign-to-me uses the captured external user id from the per-user override
Unassign calls plugin.unassignIssue
Optimistic UI revert on plugin error
Test coverage from TC-040 passes

title: GitHub.com plugin: scaffold + read-only methods (preserve githubRequest)
description: Build `plugins/github-com/` with manifest, entry, and the SDK-based plugin implementation. Implement listSourceCandidates (repos + GitHub Projects), listIssues (paginated, ETag-aware, GraphQL-batched for blocking relationships), getIssue, getAvailableTransitions, getCurrentUser, validateConfig. Preserve the existing `githubRequest` helper at `server/services/github.ts:255` verbatim inside the plugin; preserve ETag / If-None-Match short-circuiting, primary + secondary rate-limit backoff with Retry-After, 30s TTL caches, GraphQL batching for blocks/blocked-by, GitHub Projects v2 pagination, issue-type fetching.
acceptance_criteria: plugins/github-com/ ships as a bundled plugin
All read-only methods implemented
githubRequest helper preserved verbatim
ETag short-circuiting works (304 returns cached payload)
Retry-After honoured on 429 / secondary rate-limit
GraphQL batching for blocks/blockedBy returns the same data as the legacy implementation
Projects v2 pagination + issue-type fetching parity preserved
Test coverage from TC-042, TC-043 passes

title: GitHub.com plugin: OAuth deep-link + write-back methods
description: Wire the `roubo://oauth/github/callback` Electron deep link inside the github-com plugin; retain the existing OAuth flow end-to-end. Implement validateConfig + getCurrentUser via /user endpoint. Implement applyTransition (close / reopen issue) and assignIssue / unassignIssue (PATCH /repos/.../issues/:n with assignees).
acceptance_criteria: OAuth deep-link callback retained at roubo://oauth/github/callback
validateConfig returns 'Connected as <login>' on success; structured 401 on bad token
applyTransition maps to close / reopen on GitHub
assignIssue / unassignIssue update the GitHub issue's assignees array
Existing Roubo OAuth app continues to work (no client id / secret changes)
Test coverage tied to TC-037, TC-038, TC-054 (re-runs against github-com)

title: GitHub Enterprise bundled plugin (PAT + instance URL)
description: Build `plugins/ghe/` mirroring the github-com plugin but with PAT + instance URL auth. configSchema asks for instance URL + PAT + optional self-signed-TLS toggle. Reuse the same githubRequest helper (parameterized by base URL). Implement the full plugin contract (read-only + write-back).
acceptance_criteria: plugins/ghe/ ships as a bundled plugin
configSchema: instance URL (string), PAT (password), allow-self-signed-TLS (boolean)
validateConfig returns 'Connected as <login>' against a real GHE instance
All read + write methods work against a GHE host
Self-signed TLS opt-in flows end-to-end via host.fetch
Test coverage from TC-047, TC-062 passes

title: Self-hosted Jira bundled plugin (Data Center 8.14+, PAT, JQL polling, ADF→markdown, blocks mapping)
description: Build `plugins/jira-self-hosted/` with manifest, entry, full plugin contract. PAT + instance URL auth (Data Center 8.14+). Incremental polling via JQL `updated >= <last-poll-timestamp>`. ADF (Atlassian Document Format) → markdown walker for issue bodies (hand-rolled; no @atlaskit deps). Blocks / is blocked by link-type mapping configurable per project (defaults: 'blocks', 'is blocked by'). categorized-multi-list source picker shape returning Boards, Epics, Filters. Write-back: applyTransition via Jira transitions API; assignIssue via PUT /rest/api/2/issue/<key>/assignee.
acceptance_criteria: plugins/jira-self-hosted/ ships as a bundled plugin
PAT auth works on Data Center 8.14+
JQL incremental polling keyed off a stored last-poll timestamp per source
ADF → markdown walker produces readable output for body / comments
blocks / is blocked by link types map to NormalizedIssue.blocks / blockedBy; configurable names per project
applyTransition uses Jira's transitions API; allowedTransitions returned by getIssue
assignIssue updates the assignee via PUT /assignee
Source picker returns categorized-multi-list shape (Boards, Epics, Filters)
Test coverage from TC-029, TC-030, TC-048, TC-063, TC-072 passes

title: Atomic migration of existing GitHub.com projects + migration banner
description: Build `server/services/migrate.ts` runnable from `server/index.ts` boot sequence (before plugin-manager initialise). For each persisted project with a configured GitHub.com source: write a per-user override (~/.roubo/integrations/<projectId>.yaml) recording the existing GitHub Project as a selected source; enable the bundled github-com plugin; move the OAuth token from ~/.roubo/auth.json into the OS keyring slot via host.credentials.set; bump state.json schemaVersion as the SINGLE commit point; delete auth.json AFTER the bump. All file writes via `atomicWrite`. On any failure prior to the bump, roll back. Idempotent: detect a bumped schemaVersion and exit early. Render a one-time migration banner (success or rolled-back variants) at the top of the app shell; persist a dismissal marker so the banner is shown at most once forever.
acceptance_criteria: migrate.ts runs at boot before plugin-manager initialise
Per-user override written for each migrated project; roubo.yaml UNTOUCHED
OAuth token moved from auth.json into OS keyring; auth.json deleted AFTER schemaVersion bump
Bumped schemaVersion is the single commit point; rollback restores everything else if any earlier step fails
Idempotent: re-running on a bumped state is a no-op
Migration banner success / rolled-back variants render once and respect a persistent dismissal marker
Integration test boots Roubo on a fixture pre-plugin ~/.roubo and asserts post-boot shape; runs in CI
Test coverage from TC-031, TC-049, TC-068, TC-069 passes

title: Raw field handling: no PII persistence beyond active bench
description: On bench clear, strip the `raw` field from `assignedIssue` before any persistence to ~/.roubo/state.json. Document the plugin-author contract: `raw` is plugin-scoped and ephemeral; plugins MUST NOT include PII unless functionally required. Audit `server/services/state.ts` write paths to enforce this.
acceptance_criteria: Bench clear removes raw from any persisted record
state.json scrubbed of raw for cleared benches
Plugin author docs include the raw-field contract
Test coverage from TC-045 passes

title: Forward-compat paper sketch for AI-agent + project-component plugin kinds
description: Write a one-page sketch in `.specifications/integration-plugins/forward-compat-sketch.md` showing what AI-agent and project-component plugin manifests + method sets would look like. Verify the host API surface designed in this slug can host them without a major-version bump. Reference the sketch from `architecture.md`. Reviewed and approved before `hostApiVersion` 1.0.0 freeze (build-time gate).
acceptance_criteria: forward-compat-sketch.md exists at .specifications/integration-plugins/forward-compat-sketch.md
Sketch covers AI-agent plugin manifest fields + methods
Sketch covers project-component plugin manifest fields + methods
Sketch confirms (or flags deviations from) the current host API surface
architecture.md references the sketch
Reviewed before hostApiVersion 1.0.0 freeze
Test coverage from TC-041 (existence + reference) passes; TC-084 covers later validation

title: Accessibility audit across plugin surfaces
description: Cross-cutting accessibility audit: keyboard navigation, focus management on dialog open / close, screen-reader semantics on permissions dialogs, source pickers, transition dropdowns, log viewer. Surfaces audited: Plugins page (WU-010), Install dialog (WU-011), Configure dialog (WU-012), Issue source tile + Switch integration (WU-013), Source picker (WU-014), Missing-plugin prompt (WU-015), Transition dropdown (WU-018), Assign control (WU-019). Capture any regressions vs the existing Roubo accessibility baseline and fix in this WU.
acceptance_criteria: All listed surfaces are keyboard-navigable end to end
Focus management on Dialog open / close: focus moves to first interactive element on open, returns to trigger on close
Permissions Dialog announces role, title, and each permission category to a screen reader
Source picker categorized-multi-list announces tab + selection state
No accessibility regressions vs the existing Roubo baseline (measured via the existing Vitest + React Testing Library a11y checks)
Test coverage from TC-046, TC-075, TC-076 passes

title: GitHub OAuth: add security_events scope + scope detector + in-process scope cache
description: Foundational layer for the 2026-05-24 security & quality alerts addendum. Updates the Roubo GitHub OAuth app's scope set to include `security_events`. Existing tokens stay valid for regular Issues. Adds a token-scope detector that parses `X-OAuth-Scopes` from any authenticated REST response into a discriminated union (`granted`, `lacking`, `unknown`). Caches the result in-process keyed by a SHA-256 fingerprint of the token, with a 5-minute TTL, invalidated on OAuth re-consent and on stored-token replacement. No persistence to disk. The cache lives in the future `plugins/_shared-github/` package (created in WU-029) but the OAuth-app config change and the unit tests for the detector can land independently in this WU.
acceptance_criteria: Roubo GitHub OAuth app's manifest lists `security_events` alongside the existing scopes
A user with an existing token (without security_events) can launch Roubo and use regular Issues without any re-consent prompt
Calling the scope detector with a response carrying `X-OAuth-Scopes: repo, read:org` and probing for `security_events` returns `kind: lacking`
Calling the scope detector with a response carrying `X-OAuth-Scopes: repo, security_events, read:org` returns `kind: granted`
Calling the scope detector with a response that omits `X-OAuth-Scopes` entirely returns `kind: unknown` (NFR-015 graceful path)
Cache hit within 5 minutes for the same token fingerprint does NOT re-call the detector
Cache miss after 5 minutes re-evaluates
Invalidating the cache for a token id removes only that entry
Scope cache and detector are not persisted to disk; in-memory only
Plugin stdout / stderr never contains a raw token; only fingerprints

title: plugins/_shared-github/ workspace: alert fetchers + mapper + redaction + external-id formatter
description: Creates the `plugins/_shared-github/` workspace package consumed by both bundled GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise plugins. Contains: (1) three alert fetcher functions, one per category, that take a `host.fetch`-style transport and an owner/repo and return paginated open alerts; (2) an alert-to-normalized mapper that produces the new `issueType` strings (`security-code-scanning`, `security-secret-scanning`, `security-dependabot`) and tucks alert-specific metadata into `raw` after redaction; (3) raw redaction functions: Secret Scanning secret value redacted to first 4 chars + marker, Code Scanning embedded code snippet stripped to file path + line number; (4) external-id formatter that produces `<owner>/<repo>#<category>-<alert_number>` per FR-044; (5) the discriminated-union parser for the new external-id format. Includes unit tests against recorded REST fixtures per category. NOT exposed via the public plugin SDK.
acceptance_criteria: A new workspace package exists at plugins/_shared-github/ with its own package.json
The package is NOT listed as an export of plugin-sdk/
The three fetcher functions paginate via the same Link-header loop pattern used by the existing githubRequest helper
The redaction unit tests against fixtures verify the secret value is stripped from Secret Scanning raw, and the snippet is stripped from Code Scanning raw
Plugin stdout / stderr capture during the unit tests contains neither the secret nor the snippet
External-id formatter produces `<owner>/<repo>#code-scanning-<n>`, `<owner>/<repo>#secret-scanning-<n>`, `<owner>/<repo>#dependabot-<n>` exactly
The format remains stable when the input alert object is re-fetched (no time-dependent fields used)
Mapper produces `allowedTransitions: []` and `assignees: []` on every alert per FR-048

title: GitHub.com plugin: per-source security category booleans, listIssues merge, inline warnings, Configure warning chip
description: Wires the addendum into the bundled GitHub.com plugin. Adds three per-source booleans to the plugin's configSchema (and the manifest's documented config shape). Threads them through the active-config resolver so the effective per-source object carries booleans the listIssues method reads. Extends listIssues to invoke the WU-029 fetchers for any enabled category on each source, merge results with regular Issues, and emit per-source per-category warnings inline on the `ListIssuesResult` envelope (new optional `warnings` field). Adds a `Security & quality alerts` disclosure section in the Configure dialog with three React Aria Checkbox rows per source, and renders a per-category warning chip when the host receives a warning for that source/category. Disclosure label surfaces a chip-count when at least one category is on. GHAS HTTP-code → cause-string mapping table is implemented per the architecture addendum.
acceptance_criteria: Configure dialog renders the three checkboxes per source, defaulting off
Toggling a checkbox on/off persists to the per-user override file (NOT roubo.yaml) and survives a Roubo restart
listIssues with all three categories on and a healthy public repo returns regular Issues + Code Scanning + Secret Scanning + Dependabot alerts merged into one array
Each alert carries its issueType ('security-code-scanning', 'security-secret-scanning', 'security-dependabot') and the namespaced externalId per FR-044
When the Code Scanning endpoint returns 404, the warning chip appears with cause 'Code Scanning unavailable: GHAS not enabled on this repo.' and the other categories continue to fetch
When the Secret Scanning endpoint returns 451, the chip cause is 'Secret Scanning unavailable: requires GitHub Advanced Security on private repos.'
Warning chip is keyboard-reachable, has an accessible name, and clears on the next successful pull for that category
Per-category fetch failures never cause listIssues to throw — the warnings envelope is the failure surface
Source-row disclosure label shows '(Code Scanning, Dependabot)' style chip-count when categories are on

title: OAuth re-consent inline action: chip-as-button wired through existing roubo://oauth/github/callback
description: Adds the inline re-consent affordance to the per-category warning chip when the cause is a missing OAuth scope. When the user clicks the chip, Roubo opens a React Aria Dialog with upgrade copy, then opens the OAuth authorize URL in the system browser using the existing `roubo://oauth/github/callback` deep link. On successful callback, the stored token is replaced in the keyring slot in place, the token-scope cache is invalidated for the old fingerprint, and the chip re-probes on the next listIssues pull. On user cancel or callback failure, the chip reverts to its unauthenticated cause with a Retry link inside. Configure dialog must stay open through the round-trip — dialog-local React state tracks the in-flight OAuth phase (idle / connecting / waiting-for-browser / success / error).
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the chip when cause is 'token lacks security_events permission' opens the OAuth re-consent Dialog
Continuing through the dialog opens the system browser at the GitHub authorize URL with the new scope appended
Successful callback replaces the stored token in keyring slot (old token no longer valid after this point)
Token-scope cache is invalidated for the old token fingerprint immediately on callback receipt
Configure dialog remains open and interactive throughout the OAuth round-trip; closing it mid-flight does not crash the app
After successful re-consent, the next listIssues pull for the source returns alerts from the newly authorized category
Cancelling the OAuth flow leaves the chip in its previous unauthenticated state plus a Retry link
State transitions are announced via a React Aria live region for screen readers per NFR-014
The OAuth authorize URL is never written to plugin logs

title: GHE plugin parity: same wiring + PAT regeneration warning chip variant
description: Brings the GHE bundled plugin to parity with the GitHub.com plugin for the addendum. Imports the same `plugins/_shared-github/` fetchers and mapper as github.com. Threads per-source category booleans through the GHE plugin's configSchema and active-config resolver. Extends GHE listIssues to fetch alerts the same way. Because GHE uses PATs and not OAuth, the warning chip variant for missing `security_events` becomes a plain link to the GHE instance's `Personal access tokens` settings page with copy guiding the user to regenerate with the correct permission and paste back into the existing Personal access token field. Fine-grained-PAT graceful copy (NFR-015) lives in this WU's mapping table because fine-grained PATs are GHE's most common PAT shape.
acceptance_criteria: GHE plugin imports the alert fetchers from plugins/_shared-github/ (does NOT duplicate the implementation)
GHE Configure dialog renders the same three-checkbox section per source
GHE listIssues with categories enabled returns alerts merged the same way as github.com
External-ids use the same `<owner>/<repo>#<category>-<n>` format
When the GHE PAT lacks security_events, the warning chip renders 'Open token settings on <GHE_URL>' with a tooltip explaining what to do

When the GHE PAT is fine-grained (no X-OAuth-Scopes header), the chip renders the graceful copy 'Unable to verify token scopes. If category data is missing, regenerate your token with the security alert permission.' per NFR-015
Pasting a regenerated PAT into the existing PAT field on Save invalidates the scope cache and triggers a probe on the next listIssues pull

title: Issues list category chip + bench Transition/Assign suppression for alert-backed benches
description: UI changes for the Issues list and bench detail view. In IssueQueuePanel and IssuePickerModal, renders a category chip to the left of the title for issues whose normalized issueType is one of the three security categories. Chip variants: 'CodeQL' (slate), 'Secret' (amber), 'Dependabot' (blue-gray). In the bench detail view, when the assignedIssue.issueType is one of the three categories, hides the Transition dropdown entirely and renders a muted explanatory line in its place; the Assign / Unassign control is rendered but disabled with the documented tooltip. Regular Issues remain unchanged (no chip).
acceptance_criteria: Issues list chip renders only for issues whose issueType matches one of the three security categories
Chip tooltip shows the alert's externalId for correlation with the GitHub UI
Bench detail view hides the Transition dropdown for alert-backed benches (does NOT render as disabled-and-greyed)
Bench detail view shows the muted Resolved-by-pushing-code line in the Transition position
Assign / Unassign is rendered DISABLED for alert-backed benches with the documented tooltip on hover
All other bench affordances (start, stop, clear, logs, terminal, blueprint selection) work identically to regular Issues
Test coverage TC-095 passes

title: Test connection: per-category parallel probes with result strip extension
description: Extends the existing Test connection result strip in the Configure dialog. When the source has at least one alert category enabled, after `validateConfig` returns, the host runs per-category probes in parallel via `Promise.allSettled` with a 5s per-probe cap and a 12s overall budget. Each probe is a `per_page=1` request to the category endpoint. The result strip renders the existing connection-result rows plus one row per enabled category showing ok / unavailable + cause / timed-out. Timeouts on a category probe surface as 'Timed out' but do not fail the overall connection test.
acceptance_criteria: Test connection on a source with all categories on probes all three endpoints in parallel
Each per-category probe respects the 5-second per-probe cap
Total wall-clock for the strip stays within the 12-second overall budget
Per-category rows render with ok (green check) / unavailable + cause (amber warning) / Timed out (amber warning)
Disabled categories do not appear in the strip
An individual probe timeout does not fail the overall connection test — the `Connected as @<user>` row still shows green if validateConfig succeeded
Test coverage TC-094, TC-103 passes

title: Blueprint-by-issue-type extension + frozen-snapshot tolerance for alert-backed benches
description: Two small wirings. (1) Extends the blueprint-by-issue-type resolver and the mapping UI's candidate-types list to include the three new issue type strings (`security-code-scanning`, `security-secret-scanning`, `security-dependabot`). No new resolver logic — the existing matcher just needs to know the candidates. (2) Verifies the BenchManager snapshot-read path tolerates an `assignedIssue.issueType` value that no longer matches any currently-fetched alert (when the user later toggles the category off). Adds a smoke test and, if necessary, a defensive guard so the bench renders normally with its frozen snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: Blueprint-by-issue-type UI lists the three security categories as selectable mapping targets
Mapping a security category to a blueprint persists and is honored when a bench is created from an alert in that category
Toggling a category off does NOT mutate, clear, or relabel existing alert-backed benches in that category
The Configure dialog source row shows the muted help text '<K> existing benches still show alerts from this category.' when applicable
Test coverage TC-096, TC-097 passes

title: Performance + scale + accessibility hardening for the addendum surfaces
description: Final hardening pass for the 2026-05-24 addendum. (1) Performance: instruments the listIssues pull with per-category fetch timings and the ETag/304 hit ratio; verifies the NFR-013 p95 8s bar against a 5 sources × 200 items fixture and the +10% regression bar for the alerts-disabled baseline. (2) Scale: 100 sequential warm pulls (TC-107) verifying the 304 short-circuit hit rate. (3) Accessibility: axe-core scan across the new warning chip, OAuth re-consent dialog, Test connection per-category rows, Issues-list category chip, and bench Transition-hidden / Assign-disabled state. Fix any zero-serious-violations gaps. (4) Telemetry: per-category fetch counters, warning surface emission count by cause string (no PII), OAuth re-consent invocation counter — surfaced in observability per the architecture addendum.
acceptance_criteria: Performance harness fixture covers 5 sources × 200 items with all categories on; p95 < 8000ms over 30 warm runs
Issues-only baseline run records p95; alerts-disabled p95 is within 10% of baseline
Scale harness (100 warm pulls) demonstrates >= 90% 304 short-circuit hit rate on unchanged endpoints
axe-core scan of the addendum surfaces reports zero serious violations
Per-category fetch timings appear in observability per the architecture addendum
Warning emission counts are tagged with cause-string category only (never with token, user id, or repo name when in private telemetry)
OAuth re-consent invocation counter ticks once per successful re-consent
Test coverage TC-098, TC-099, TC-107 passes
